summary: A legendary Merriweather storm show with a soaring Sugar Magnolia and thunder‑lit Wharf Rat, captured on an audience tape that is surprisingly clear despite some tinny sections.
review: Fans describe this June 1983 Merriweather Post Pavilion gig as one of the Dead’s most intense performances – the second set shines with a lightning‑charged Wharf Rat, a monster Sugar Magnolia and relentless Music Never Stopped. The recording is an audience (AUD) source; while some sections sound tinny or hollow, the overall mix is good enough to enjoy the historic moment.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly praise the musical performance (standout second set, iconic songs) and give high star ratings; recording issues are noted but not enough to outweigh the show’s greatness.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear for an audience tape, but post‑drums channel is tinny/distorted, occasional hollowness and drop‑outs; overall listenable and better than most AUDs of the era.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid and rain‑soaked, second set legendary with the storm syncing to the band; seamless transitions especially into Wharf Rat and a climactic Sugar Magnolia.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Powerful guitar work throughout – especially on Sugar Magnolia and during lightning moments; a few note a botched bridge in China Cat Sunflower.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bomb‑dropping bass that rode the thunder, highlighted during Wharf Rat and the second‑set jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Raps wildly on Sugar Magnolia’s “Dreamtime” section, hammering his guitar on stage and delivering intense vocals on The Other One.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) steps forward with ferocious fills, giving a taste of his later work.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey & Bill drive the stormy energy; drums noted as relentless and “relentless” by reviewers.
